calendar.matters:
Petitioner Noble Fields School Of Real Estate, Llc'S Petition For Writ Of Adminstrative Mandate
Off Calendar Jan-21-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Adam Slote and Marglyn Paseka of Slote Links & Boreman, LLP telephone number 415-393-8001 for Petitioner.
Lucy Wang of California Attorney General telephone number 415-703-5202 for Respondent Bureau of Real Estate.

The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling. Petitioner Noble Fields School Of Real Estate, LLC's Verified Petition For Writ Of Adminstrative Mandate, Stay of Adminstrative Order and Attorney's Fees is denied. Under CCP section 1094.5, the petitioner must show that the findings of the administrative agency are not supported by substantial evidence in the record. (Shapell Industries, Inc. v. Governing Board (1991) 1 Cal.App.4th 218, 231.) The independent judgment standard does not apply to the case at hand because a vested right is not at issue. Ms. Fields' license is not being disciplined and Petitioner is still allowed to function as a school by offering statutory courses. Pursuant to California Code of Regulations, Tile 10, section 3010. subdivision (a), the Bureau of Real Estate properly exercised its authority to withdraw approval for Petitioner to offer continuing education courses based on Petitioner's failure to comply with applicable standards. The ALJ determined the findings of fact and found that the school failed to provide course information in violation of California Code of Regulations, title 10, section 3007, subdivision (f), the school failed to administer multiple exams that had been approved by the Bureau of Real Estate in meeting applicable standards in violation of California Code of Regulations, title 10, section 3007.3, subdivision (d)(3), (4), and the school offered a course that has expired in violation of California Code of Regulations, title 10, section 3007.3, subdivision (d)(5) and (e). (Administrative Record 833-838.) Petitioner does not dispute that the violations occurred. Petitioner has not established that the findings of the administrative agency are not supported by substantial evidence in the record. The willful disregard standard that Petitioner cites to is inapplicable in the instant case because that standard only applies to discipline actions taken against those with real estate salesperson and broker licenses. (Bus. and Prof. Code section 10177(d).) Petitioner does not cite to any relevant authority to support its position that the willful disregard standard also applies to actions taken against a real estate school. Additionally, the notice provided by the Bureau of Real Estate was sufficient as it notified Petitioner that the Bureau of Real Estate sought to withdraw approval of all continuing education courses which had either been approved or were in the process of being approved. The notice effectively informed Petitioner that it would not be able to offer any continuing education courses. Order signed in open Court.

Judge: Ernest Goldsmith. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/EG)
